channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Rangers blink but twists still to come in colossal title race
item.description: This epic Scottish Premiership season won't be over until it's over, writes Tom English.

item.title: Man Utd survive Brentford fight back to earn crucial win
item.description: Manchester United hold their nerve as first-half goals from Casemiro and Benjamin Sesko are enough for them to win an entertaining encounter with Brentford.

item.title: Arsenal expect Havertz to play in title run-in
item.description: Arsenal expect Kai Havertz to play a part in their title run-in despite the forward suffering a muscular injury against Newcastle.

item.title: Who will win the Champions League? Place your vote
item.description: Thirty-two teams have fallen in this season's Champions League and four remain - so who will become kings of Europe on 30 May? BBC Sport delves into the stats, while experts make predictions.

item.title: World Cup group stage set for yellow card amnesty
item.description: Fifa is set to change the rules around suspensions for accumulated yellow cards at this summer's World Cup.

item.title: Could a Premier League club lose their European place once again?
item.description: After Crystal Palace's issues last season, could a Premier League club lose their European place once again because of dual ownership issues?

item.title: Newcastle to explore selling Wissa - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Newcastle will explore selling Yoane Wissa in the summer, Liam Delap wants to stay and fight for his Chelsea career, Barcelona contact Atletico Madrid over a possible transfer for Julian Alvarez, plus more.

item.title: Spurs' Simons to miss rest of season and World Cup
item.description: Tottenham and Netherlands attacking midfielder Xavi Simons is "heartbroken" to have injured his anterior cruciate ligament.

item.title: Four ways to tackle football's frustrating 'tactical timeout'
item.description: Next season, leagues will be allowed to select from a series of trials to try to stop the goalkeeper tactical time-out. BBC Sport looks at the proposals.

item.title: Man City discuss fixture scheduling with Premier League
item.description: Manchester City are in discussions with the Premier League over the scheduling of two fixtures that could prove crucial in the title race.

item.title: Champions League at risk for Man Utd so what next for Skinner?
item.description: Securing Women's Champions League football for next season is now out of Manchester United's hands - so where does that leave manager Marc Skinner?

item.title: Carrick hails 'big three points' after beating Brentford
item.description: Manchester United interim boss Michael Carrick says that his side's goal is "to finish as high up the league as possible" following their 2-1 win against Brentford at Old Trafford.

item.title: Andrews 'bitterly disappointed' after Brentford lose at Man Utd
item.description: Brentford boss Keith Andrews says his side aren't "playing fearful" football and that he doesn't want to be that sort of team after a 2-1 loss to Manchester United in the Premier League.

item.title: Fernandez fires Chelsea to FA Cup final showdown with Man City
item.description: Enzo Fernandez' first-half header gives Chelsea a 1-0 win over Leeds United in the semi-final of the FA Cup at Wembley Stadium.

item.title: Lingard on playing in Brazil and Man Utd
item.description: Jesse Lingard talks about moving to Corinthians, and discusses why Michael Carrick should get the Manchester United manager's job.

item.title: How lack of goalline technology denied Kerr hat-trick
item.description: Women's Football Show pundits Gilly Flaherty and Nia Jones look at Sam Kerr's legitimate goal that wasn't given due to a lack of goalline technology in the WSL.

item.title: Making history and facing Neymar - Lingard on life in Brazil
item.description: In his first major interview since moving to Brazil, Jesse Lingard talks to the BBC about his new life, and Manchester United.

item.title: 'Pure ecstasy' - York and a Saturday that may never be beaten
item.description: BBC Radio York broadcaster David Ward on York City's promotion, passion and a priceless piece of commentary.

item.title: Southampton and Ipswich face automatic promotion showdown
item.description: Southampton must beat Ipswich to take their Championship promotion push to the final day, but a win for Town would see them all but back in the Premier League.

item.title: Real striker Mbappe a doubt for El Clasico
item.description: Real Madrid striker Kylian Mbappe is a doubt for the El Clasico against rivals Barcelona next month because of a hamstring injury.

item.title: Could Newcastle get dragged into relegation fight?
item.description: Newcastle have lost their past five games in all competitions and are eight points clear of the Premier League bottom three.

item.title: Braga, Just, Maswanhise & Shankland up for award
item.description: Hearts forwards Claudio Braga and Lawrence Shankland and Motherwell attackers Elijah Just and Tawanda Maswanhise are the four candidates for PFA Scotland's player of the year award.

item.title: 'Complete loss of control' - keeper punches player after being sent off
item.description: Real Zaragoza goalkeeper Esteban Andrada punched Huesca captain Jorge Pulido after being sent off in a heated Spanish second division game.

item.title: Arsenal capitalise on defensive errors to secure first leg win over Lyon
item.description: Arsenal pounce on a pair of defensive errors to come from behind to defeat Lyon 2-1 at the Emirates Stadium, in the first leg of their Women's Champions League semi-final.

item.title: Tactical stoppages need clamping down on - Murphy
item.description: BBC Sport pundit Danny Murphy believes Chelsea utilised "an obvious tactical stoppage" in their FA Cup semi-final win against Leeds United, and proposes rule changes that can prevent it from happening.

item.title: Record-equalling Kerr helps Chelsea dominate Everton
item.description: Sam Kerr scores twice to join former teammate Fran Kirby in being Chelsea's joint all-time top goalscorer in the WSL, as Sonia Bompastor's side soundly defeat Everton 4-1 at Goodison Park.

item.title: Williamson calls for Arsenal 'intensity' in second leg
item.description: Leah Williamson believes Arsenal will have to be "really clinical" and "defensively secure" if they are to maintain their lead in the second leg of their Women's Champions League semi-final away to Lyon.

item.title: Watch: League 1 title race heats up & East Kilbride near promotion
item.description: Watch the best of the action from the weekend's action in the Scottish Championship, League 1 and League 2.

item.title: Quiz: Can you name every FA Cup finalist since 2010?
item.description: From the underdogs to the serial winners, can you name every FA Cup finalist since 2010?

item.title: Motherwell rediscover mojo to have say in title race
item.description: Hearts and Celtic beware as Motherwell find their mojo again to have say in Scottish Premiership title race, writes Kheredine Idessane.

item.title: Fishlock awaits injury news after stretcher exit
item.description: Former Wales star Jess Fishlock is awaiting a medical verdict for an injury that led to her being carried off on a stretcher for Seattle Reign.

item.title: Hire, fire, final - Chelsea's player power may be ugly, but it wins
item.description: Chelsea are in another FA Cup final - but only after their players showed up in a way they were no longer doing for Liam Rosenior, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: Arsenal 'on a mission' - is another special season on the horizon?
item.description: The Gunners are one game away from a second successive Women's Champions League final, and are also still in the Women's Super League title race.

item.title: Win or bust for Rangers as Tynecastle trip looms large
item.description: Defeat by Motherwell means Rangers' next game away to Hearts is win or bust for Danny Rohl's side, writes Kheredine Idessane.

item.title: 'Four days of hell in hospital' - Kearns on miscarriage
item.description: Aston Villa and England midfielder Missy Bo Kearns says she spent "four days of hell in hospital" after suffering a miscarriage and contracting sepsis.

item.title: London City thrash WSL bottom side Leicester
item.description: London City Lionesses come from behind to inflict a damaging 5-1 defeat on bottom of the table Leicester City in the Women's Super League.

item.title: Smith capitalises on defensive mix-up from Lyon
item.description: OL Lyonnes goalkeeper Christiane Endler is involved in another mix-up at the back against Arsenal as she crashes into Ingrid Syrstad Engen to allow Olivia Smith to give the Gunners the lead in the first leg of their Women's Champions League semi-final tie.

item.title: Man City beat Southampton to reach FA Cup final
item.description: Manchester City score two goals in five minutes to secure a late comeback victory against Southampton and reach their fourth FA Cup final in a row.

item.title: Crucial win over Liverpool lifts West Ham away from relegation danger
item.description: A Cornelia Kapocs own goal is enough for West Ham to secure a 1-0 away win over Liverpool, as the East London side move seven points clear of the relegation play-off place in the WSL.

item.title: Superb Eze effort enough to see Arsenal beat Newcastle
item.description: An early Eberechi Eze strike proves to be the difference as Arsenal hold on to beat Newcastle United 1-0 at the Emirates Stadium and move back to the top of the Premier League table.

item.title: Palhinha scores crucial goal to boost Spurs' survival hopes
item.description: Joao Palhinha's late goal earns Tottenham a vital 1-0 win over Wolves at Molineux, to keep themselves just two points behind relegation rivals West Ham United in the Premier League.

item.title: Galatasaray accuse referees of having 'evil inside' them
item.description: Galatasaray accuse referees in Turkey of having "evil inside" them just days after suspending relations with the Turkish Football Federation.

item.title: How 10 Premier League teams can qualify for Europe
item.description: Ten Premier League teams in Europe? It sounds preposterous, but it is not as far-fetched as it seems.

item.title: Italian referee chief suspends himself over allegations
item.description: The man in charge of assigning referees in Italy's top flight suspends himself after it is alleged he is under investigation for sporting fraud.

item.title: Will doubters believe in Hearts' title chances now?
item.description: Some have waited all season for Hearts to falter. Plenty thought it might be at Easter Road. But Derek McInnes' side are still going, writes Tom English.

item.title: Why Rashford must be patient over Barcelona future
item.description: Marcus Rashford is not ready to give up on his Barcelona dream just yet, writes Spanish football expert Guillem Balague.

item.title: Three into two - battle to reach WSL goes to final day
item.description: Just one matchday remains in the WSL 2 and Birmingham City, Crystal Palace and Charlton Athletic could all secure automatic promotion.

item.title: Police 'aware' of video appearing to show York player punching fan
item.description: Police say they are aware of a video circulating online that appears to show a York City player punching a fan during the club's dramatic 1-1 draw with Rochdale on Saturday.

item.title: Three goals, eight chaotic minutes - how day of relegation drama unfolded
item.description: On a day of twists and turns, Tottenham and West Ham both picked up crucial wins in their fight to avoid relegation.

item.title: Forest Green deny fan was ejected for bacon butty
item.description: Forest Green Rovers deny claims a supporter was ejected from their stadium on Saturday for eating a bacon sandwich.

item.title: Their day, until it wasn't - the title decider that had it all
item.description: Two goals in stoppage time and pitch invasions by both sets of fans. The last day of the National League season lived up to its billing.

item.title: Trophyless to treble? Man City's quest for more history
item.description: Manchester City were in a state of turmoil this time last year – staring at a first trophyless season since 2017 – but they are now in with a chance of a domestic treble.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: Kinsky's save, Palhinha's relief and - finally - a Tottenham win
item.description: Tottenham end their long wait for a Premier League win with victory at Wolves - lifting their survival hopes in the process.

item.title: Why is Kelly not an automatic starter for Arsenal or England?
item.description: BBC Sport discusses why Lionesses hero Chloe Kelly is not guaranteed a spot for club or country.

item.title: Why I stayed at 'struggling' Man Utd and didn't think we'd be third now - Fernandes
item.description: Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes speaks to club legend Wayne Rooney in a wide-ranging interview.

item.title: The remarkable rise of Chelsea's little-known interim boss
item.description: Chelsea's interim head coach Calum McFarlane admits this year has been a "whirlwind", going from obscurity to an FA Cup semi‑final at Wembley.

item.title: World Cup 2026 injury watch: Key names racing to be ready
item.description: After Spain's Lamine Yamal was ruled out of the rest of his club season, BBC Sport looks at other key names racing to be fit for the World Cup - and those who are set to miss out through injury.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: Who can still receive a yellow card suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Goodwin expects transfer interest in Ferry - gossip
item.description: Dundee United boss expects interest in left-back, Rangers head coach cites penalty incident and John McGinn on Everton's radar.

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: What's on the line as WSL reaches business end?
item.description: BBC Sport analyses the state of play in the Women's Super League with only a handful of games to go and the title, European qualification, relegation and promotion still to be decided.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
